# André Bowen

> Australian oil painter working in still life, landscape, and the figure. Based in the Northern Rivers of New South Wales.

André Bowen (born 1974, Australia) studied at the National Art School in Sydney, the UNSW College of Fine Arts, and the Art Students League of New York. He spent a decade living and painting in Leipzig and Berlin before returning to Australia. His work is held in private collections in Australia, the United States, and Europe.

## Full content

https://andrebowen.com/llms-full.txt

## Sitemap

https://andrebowen.com/sitemap.xml

## Pages

- [Still Life](https://andrebowen.com/still-life.html): Oil paintings of objects observed closely, painted from life in natural light.
- [Landscape](https://andrebowen.com/landscape.html): Plein-air paintings made on location in the Northern Rivers of New South Wales, New Zealand's South Island, and Berlin.
- [Figure](https://andrebowen.com/figure.html): Oil paintings of the human figure.
- [Drawings](https://andrebowen.com/drawings.html): Portraits, figure studies, and plein-air sketches.
- [About](https://andrebowen.com/about.html): Biography, exhibition history, awards, residencies, and collections.
- [Contact](https://andrebowen.com/contact.html): Enquiries regarding available works, exhibitions, or commissions.

## Selected exhibitions

- 2019: Solo exhibition, Defiance Gallery at Mary Place, Paddington, Sydney
- 2018: Paddington Art Prize — winner, Defiance Gallery Prize
- 2013: Doug Moran National Portrait Prize
- 2010: Solo exhibition, Defiance Gallery, Paddington, Sydney
- 2010: A Perfect Day to Chase Tornadoes, Bethanien House, Berlin
- 1998: Chinese Theatre, Carlton Arms Hotel, New York

## Collections

- ICAA, Sydney
- The Art Cannot Be Damaged, New York
- The Molongo Collection, Canberra

## Bibliography

- 2023: *Nathan* — Marie Eve Nadeau (front cover artwork)
- 2018: *Bangalow Herald*
- 2016: *Black Night, Black Knight* — Mike Tyler (front cover artwork)
- 2006: *New Blood* — Australian Art Review, March 2006
- 2002: *New York Hotel Story* — Nathalie Daoust
